records.ety: [L. concussio, fr. concutere, concussum, to shake violenty; con- + quatere to shake. See Cashier, Quash.]

defs: A shaking or agitation; a shock; caused by the collision of two bodies.
quotes: It is believed that great ringing of bells, in populous cities, hath dissipated pestilent air; which may be from the concussion of the air.

defs: A condition of lowered functional activity, without visible structural change, produced in an organ by a shock, as by fall or blow; as, a concussion of the brain.
The unlawful forcing of another by threats of violence to yield up something of value.
quotes: Then concussion, rapine, pilleries, Their catalogue of accusations fill.
